welcome.title: Welcome to Election Sahayak
welcome.description: Your personal guide to India's electoral process. Enter your age and registration status to get started.
welcome.f1Desc: Content tailored to your voter eligibility and registration status.
welcome.f2Desc: Ask any question about elections, voting, and registration.
welcome.f3Desc: Locate your nearest Electoral Registration Office on the map.
welcome.f4Desc: Add election dates and deadlines to your Google Calendar.

civics.title: Civics 101
civics.subtitle: Understanding India's Democracy
civics.eligibleIn: You will be eligible to vote in
civics.alreadyEligible: You are already age-eligible to vote!
civics.c1Desc: Democracy is a system of government where citizens exercise power by voting. India is the world's largest democracy with over 900 million eligible voters.
civics.c2Desc: India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVM) and VVPAT system. Elections are conducted by the Election Commission of India (ECI), an independent constitutional body.
civics.c3Desc: Every vote counts! In past elections, some seats were decided by fewer than 100 votes. Your participation shapes the future of your community and country.
civics.c4Desc: The ECI is responsible for administering election processes. It ensures free and fair elections across all levels — from Panchayat to Parliament.
civics.prepDesc: Start gathering the documents you'll need when you turn 18:

registration.title: Register to Vote
registration.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to voter registration
registration.s1d: You'll need: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size photo, Proof of address (utility bill, bank statement, or rent agreement).
registration.s2d: Visit the National Voter Service Portal (NVSP) at voters.eci.gov.in to fill Form 6 online.
registration.s3d: Submit your application at your nearest Electoral Registration Office. Use the map below to find one.
registration.s4d: Use your reference number on the NVSP portal to track your application status.
registration.s5d: Once approved, your Electoral Photo Identity Card will be delivered or available for collection at the ERO.
registration.eroDesc: Enter your PIN code to locate the nearest Electoral Registration Office.
registration.calDesc: Don't forget to register! Add a reminder to your Google Calendar.

polling.title: Your Polling Day Guide
polling.subtitle: Everything you need for Election Day
polling.t1: 7:00 AM — Polling begins. Arrive early to avoid long queues.
polling.t2: 7:00–9:00 AM — Best time to vote — shortest wait times.
polling.t3: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM — Regular voting hours. Carry water and snacks.
polling.t4: 6:00 PM — Polling ends. Everyone in queue by 6 PM can still vote.
polling.i2: Any additional photo ID (Aadhaar, Passport, etc.)
polling.i3: Know your Polling Booth number and serial number
polling.e1: The presiding officer will verify your identity and ink your finger.
polling.e2: Enter the voting compartment. You'll see the EVM with candidate names and symbols.
polling.e3: Press the blue button next to your chosen candidate. A beep confirms your vote.
polling.e4: Check the VVPAT slip (visible for 7 seconds) to verify your vote.
polling.e5: Exit the booth. Do not discuss your vote inside the polling station.
polling.boothDesc: Enter your PIN code to find the nearest polling booth.
